name: HDC-1A "Frostbite"
description: The baseline heat drill capture unit, the Frostbite is a compact thermal penetrator system that drives a resistive heating element directly into ice-bearing regolith. A nichrome alloy coil at the drill tip reaches 600 K, sublimating water ice on contact. The resulting vapor rises through an annular gap between the drill shaft and a sealed borehole sleeve into an umbrella-shaped capture hood, where a Stirling-cycle cryocondenser chills it back to liquid water for collection in an insulated sump tank. At 2.5 tonnes, the Frostbite is light enough to fit in any medium cargo bay — the drill assembly, capture hood, and condenser together mass less than a single centrifugal drum from the sifting line. The tradeoff is power: 1.5 MW of continuous electrical draw feeds the heating element, and that energy must come from somewhere. Designed for permanently shadowed craters and other ice-rich sites where water concentration exceeds 8%.
